office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: The incumbent emphasizes policing and tax restraint, a recently elected charter commissioner focuses on household costs and housing protections, and an outsider candidate offers broad fiscal-sustainability themes.

candidates.summary: Jacobsen is the incumbent representative and former councilmember emphasizing police resources, responses to homelessness and addiction, jobs, traffic, and opposition to new taxes.
highlights: Supports police funding, recruitment, and retention [S1]

safety.basis: She emphasizes police funding, recruitment, and retention alongside treatment-related priorities.

experience.basis: She is the incumbent representative and a former city councilmember.

candidates.summary: Strickling is a recently elected charter-review commissioner and small-business owner focusing on household costs, local jobs, and housing protections.
highlights: Prioritizes gas, grocery, and housing affordability [S1]
Cites charter work on housing protections for seniors and veterans [S1]

housing.basis: She cites housing protections intended to help seniors and veterans remain in their homes.

experience.basis: She has recently elected charter-review experience but no legislative record.

candidates.summary: Fanony is an architectural designer and first-time candidate focused broadly on taxes, living costs, public services, and sustainable finances.
highlights: Wants to address rising taxes and living costs while maintaining public services [S1]

experience.basis: He explicitly reports no prior public office.
